seo.title: Building AI Coding Assistant with AutoGen + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai coding assistant with AutoGen + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building an AI Coding Assistant with AutoGen enables multi-agent collaboration for code generation, debugging, and testing with repository context. However, conversational agents risk infinite loops, unsafe code execution, and incorrect solutions that could introduce vulnerabilities. Vex integration provides essential guardrails to verify code correctness, security, and compliance before deployment.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Coding Assistant Architecture with AutoGen
architectureOverview.description:
The AI Coding Assistant uses AutoGen's multi-agent system where a Code Planner agent analyzes user requirements and repository context, delegates to a Code Generator agent for implementation, and coordinates with a Tester agent for validation. All agents operate within sandboxed execution environments, iterating through debugging cycles until tests pass. Human-in-the-loop approval gates ensure final quality.

Repository context is injected via file embeddings or AST parsing, enabling agents to understand existing codebase structure. Sandbox execution prevents malicious code from affecting host systems, while test validation uses pytest or unittest frameworks. Iterative debugging handles edge cases through structured debate protocols.

Vex integrates as a reply filter across all agents, verifying code outputs against security, correctness, and compliance policies before conversation continues.

risks.name: Infinite conversation loops between agents
risks.description: Agents enter endless debate cycles without converging on solutions, exhausting tokens and compute.
risks.mitigation: Vex conversation termination policies detect loops and enforce max-turn limits

risks.name: Code execution producing unsafe outputs
risks.description: Generated code contains security vulnerabilities, credential leaks, or malicious payloads.
risks.mitigation: Vex static analysis scans for vulnerabilities, secrets, and dependency risks before execution

risks.name: Agents agreeing on incorrect conclusions
risks.description: Multiple agents validate flawed code due to hallucination consensus, bypassing human review.
risks.mitigation: Vex independent verification runs test suites and security scans regardless of agent consensus

risks.name: Uncontrolled token spending in multi-turn debates
risks.description: Verbose agent discussions exceed budget without producing actionable code improvements.
risks.mitigation: Vex token budgeting and early termination for non-progressive conversations

implementation.title: Setup AutoGen Agents with Sandbox Executor
implementation.description: Configure core agents with Docker sandbox for safe code execution and repository context injection.
implementation.code:
from autogen import AssistantAgent, UserProxyAgent, DockerCommandLineCodeExecutor
import vex

executor = DockerCommandLineCodeExecutor(work_dir="sandbox")

planner = AssistantAgent(
    name="CodePlanner",
    llm_config={"config_list": [{"model": "gpt-4", "api_key": "..."}]},
    reply_filter=vex.filter,
    code_executor=executor
)

generator = AssistantAgent(
    name="CodeGenerator",
    system_message="Generate production-ready Python code with tests.",
    reply_filter=vex.filter,
    code_executor=executor
)

tester = AssistantAgent(
    name="Tester",
    system_message="Run pytest and validate code correctness.",
    reply_filter=vex.filter,
    code_executor=executor
)

implementation.title: Repository Context Injection
implementation.description: Load repository files and inject structured context into agent conversations.
implementation.code:
import os
import ast
from pathlib import Path

def load_repo_context(repo_path: str) -> str:
    context = "Repository Structure:\n"
    for file_path in Path(repo_path).rglob("*.py"):
        try:
            source = file_path.read_text()
            tree = ast.parse(source)
            classes = [node.name for node in ast.walk(tree) if isinstance(node, ast.ClassDef)]
            context += f"{file_path}: {len(source)} chars, classes: {classes[:3]}\n"
        except:
            pass
    return context

repo_context = load_repo_context("/path/to/repo")
user_proxy.initiate_chat(
    planner,
    message=f"Fix bug in user management. Context: {repo_context}"
)

implementation.title: Iterative Debugging Workflow
implementation.description: Implement structured iteration with test feedback loops and convergence criteria.
implementation.code:
def debug_cycle(planner, generator, tester, max_iterations=5):
    for i in range(max_iterations):
        plan = planner.generate_reply()
        code = generator.generate_reply(messages=[plan])
        
        test_result = tester.generate_reply(messages=[code])
        if "ALL TESTS PASSED" in test_result:
            return code
        
        feedback = f"Iteration {i+1} failed: {test_result}"
        planner.update_messages([feedback])
    raise Exception("Max iterations exceeded")

# Execute debugging cycle
final_code = debug_cycle(planner, generator, tester)

implementation.title: Human-in-the-Loop Approval
implementation.description: Final code review gate before deployment with Vex verification summary.
implementation.code:
def human_review(code: str, vex_report: dict) -> bool:
    print("=== VEX VERIFICATION REPORT ===")
    print(f"Code Correctness: {vex_report['code_correctness']['score']}/100")
    print(f"Security Score: {vex_report['security']['score']}/100")
    print(f"Secrets Found: {vex_report['secrets']['count']}")
    print(f"License Compliance: {vex_report['license']['valid']}")
    
    approval = input("Approve for deployment? (y/n): ")
    return approval.lower() == 'y'

if human_review(final_code, vex_report):
    print("Deploying approved code...")

vexIntegration.title: Register Vex Reply Filter
vexIntegration.description: Initialize Vex SDK and register as AutoGen reply filter for all agents.
vexIntegration.code:
import vex
from autogen import register_function

# Initialize Vex with coding assistant policies
vex.init(
    api_key="your-vex-api-key",
    policies={
        "code_correctness": "high",
        "security_scanning": "critical",
        "secret_detection": "block",
        "dependency_safety": "medium",
        "license_compliance": "warn"
    }
)

# Register Vex as reply filter for all agents
def vex_filter(messages, sender, config):
    if sender and hasattr(sender, 'name'):
        result = vex.verify({
            "agent": sender.name,
            "content": messages[-1]['content'],
            "context": "coding_assistant"
        })
        return result['approved']
    return True

register_function(
vex_filter,
callable=True,
name="vex_guardrails",
description="Vex safety verification"
)
